title: Fix Dashboard card navigation and display owner/complaint on card

requirements.text: Fix the click handler on CalendarCaseCard so that clicking a card on the Dashboard correctly sets the targetCaseId state in App.tsx and navigates the user to the Cases page, scrolling to and highlighting the corresponding case card.

quotes: clicking a card on the Dashboard does not take you straight to the corresponding case on the Cases page

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking any CalendarCaseCard on the Dashboard navigates the user to the Cases page.
The Cases page scrolls to and highlights the case that was clicked.
The targetCaseId prop/state is correctly passed from CalendarCaseCard up through DashboardPage to App.tsx, triggering the page switch.
Navigation works for all cases visible on the Dashboard calendar.

requirements.text: Display the owner name and presenting complaint (from the VetCase data) on each CalendarCaseCard on the Dashboard, in addition to the existing pet name, MRN, and species/sex information.

quotes: display owner name and presenting complaint on the card on the dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: Each CalendarCaseCard shows the owner name if it is available on the case.
Each CalendarCaseCard shows the presenting complaint if it is available on the case.
Owner name and presenting complaint are legible and fit within the compact card layout without breaking the calendar grid.
If owner name or presenting complaint is absent for a case, those fields are gracefully omitted or shown as empty.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/ (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts) or frontend/src/main.tsx.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui/.
All navigation state must remain in App.tsx using the existing targetCaseId pattern.
nonGoals: Adding new backend fields or changing the VetCase data model.
Changing the Cases page filtering or sorting behavior.
